items.title: Pubs, restaurants and hotels slash bills with energy saving tool
items.summary: Pubs, restaurants and hotels slash energy bills with free energy saving and carbon reduction tool.

items.title: Britain to lead fusion energy race to deliver energy security
items.summary: Government sets out its vision for developing a commercial fusion industry in the UK that will deliver skilled jobs, investment and growth.

items.title: ILIOS appointment speeds delivery of STEP Fusion at West Burton
items.summary: The partnership with ILIOS will help ensure that West Burton becomes a leading centre of fusion energy innovation.

items.title: Policy paper: National Minimum Wage and National Living Wage: Low Pay Commission remit 2026
items.summary: The government’s remit to the Low Pay Commission (LPC) outlining areas to consider when recommending the 2027 National Living Wage and National Minimum Wage rates.

items.title: UKAEA-Eni Tritium Loop Facility expands international partnership
items.summary: Canada's Kinectrics announced as the design and fabrication partner for the UKAEA-Eni H3AT Tritium Loop Facility, a world-leading tritium research facility.

items.title: £45M for UK’s first AI supercomputer to accelerate fusion energy
items.summary: 1.4MW mission-focused supercomputer named ‘Sunrise’, primed to be the world's most powerful AI supercomputer dedicated to fusion energy.

items.title: Guidance: Horizon Convictions Redress Scheme (HCRS): legal cost framework
items.summary: This framework sets out the legal costs we will cover for those applying for financial redress under the Horizon Convictions Redress Scheme (HCRS).

items.title: Decision: Complaint to UK NCP by Possible about British Airways plc
items.summary: A complaint was filed by Possible: the 10:10 Foundation alleging that British Airways plc has made misleading statements regarding its environmental performance.

items.title: Policy paper: UK fusion strategy 2026
items.summary: A strategy to accelerate growth of the fusion industry, grow supply chains and skills, and position the UK as a global leader in commercial fusion.

items.title: Transparency data: UK-Norway, Iceland, and Liechtenstein FTA Sub-Committee on Trade and Sustainable Development (TSD): joint minutes, 11 November 2025
items.summary: Summary of the second meeting of the UK-Norway, Iceland, and Liechtenstein Free Trade Agreement Sub-Committee on Trade and Sustainable Development (TSD).

items.title: Accreditation
items.summary: Guidance for business and government departments.

items.summary: Capacity Market T-4 Auction Monitor Report for 2029 to 2030 delivery year.

items.title: Government to go "further and faster" in becoming energy secure
items.summary: The Energy Secretary outlines measures to protect consumers and make Britain energy secure.

items.title: Policy paper: Industrial Strategy Prospectus
items.summary: This document summarises the UK’s Modern Industrial Strategy and our Sector Plans, including our offer to businesses.

items.title: Guidance: Security & Policing 2026: countries, territories and organisations invited to attend by the Department for Business and Trade
items.summary: Countries, territories and organisations invited by UK Defence and Security Exports (UKDSE) to attend Security & Policing 2026.

items.title: Notice: Notice to exporters 2026/04: transmission issues between LITE/SPIRE and CDS
items.summary: Licences from LITE/SPIRE may not reach HMRC’s Customs Declaration Service (CDS), causing clearance delays. Report declaration problems to CDS while we investigate.

items.title: Notice: Notice to exporters 2026/06: resolved transmission issues with CDS
items.summary: The Export Control Joint Unit (ECJU) confirms the licence transmission issue is resolved and normal declarations may resume.

items.title: Notice: CCUS East Coast Cluster: Teesside selection process
items.summary: Apply to take part in the ECC Teesside selection process for projects seeking connection to the Teesside network by 2032.

items.title: Policy paper: Building our nuclear nation: government response to the Nuclear Regulatory Review 2025
items.summary: UK to reform nuclear regulation: streamline approvals, cut delays, boost innovation, and ensure proportionate, efficient delivery.

items.title: Overhaul of nuclear system to speed up building and cut costs
items.summary: Plan to overhaul nuclear system will speed up building, strengthen national and energy security, and cut costs.

items.title: Policy paper: UK Emissions Trading Scheme (UK ETS): policy overview
items.summary: The role of the UK Emissions Trading Scheme in UK decarbonisation and how the UK ETS Authority is developing the scheme.

items.title: UK Emissions Trading Scheme: Regulating cross-boundary CCS pipelines
items.summary: The UK ETS Authority is seeking views on making regulatory requirements for cross-boundary carbon capture and storage (CCS) pipelines less complex, burdensome and costly.

items.title: Transparency data: DBT: spending over £500, September 2025
items.summary: Spending by electronic purchasing card solution (ePCS), over £500.

items.title: Independent report: Nuclear Decommissioning Authority: 2026 review
items.summary: The government has commissioned an independent review of the Nuclear Decommissioning Authority (NDA) to assess its performance and identify opportunities for improvement.

items.summary: How we will use your personal data and what your rights are.

items.title: Make Work Pay: improving access to flexible working
items.summary: We are seeking views on a new process for employers to follow when handling flexible working requests, as well as insights on flexible working practices more broadly.

items.title: Accelerating electricity network connections for strategic demand
items.summary: We're seeking views on proposals to amend the connections process to address speculation and prioritise future capacity for strategic demand, including data centres.

items.title: Notice: Hydrogen Network Code: industry engagement
items.summary: Notification that we will shortly begin industry engagement relating to the development of a new network code for hydrogen.

items.title: Notice: Trade remedies notices: registration of imports of tin mill products originating from China
items.summary: Trade remedies notices published by the Secretary of State for Business and Trade relating to the registration of imports of tin mill products originating from China.

items.title: Notice: Heat Pump Ready Programme: successful projects
items.summary: Projects awarded under Stream 1, Stream 2 and Stream 3 of the Heat Pump Ready Programme, part of the £1 billion Net Zero Innovation Portfolio.

items.summary: Guidance for UK businesses on countering Russian sanctions evasion and circumvention.

items.summary: Funding for local authorities to deliver energy performance and low carbon heating upgrades to low-income homes in England.

items.title: Guidance: Summary of questions included in the DESNZ/BEIS Public Attitudes Tracker since Autumn 2021

items.summary: This guidance sets out the legal costs the Department for Business and Trade (DBT) will cover for Capture Redress Scheme applicants.

items.summary: Licence for the export of military goods for the production, development or maintenance of Typhoon aircraft.

items.summary: Reports on how redress in the Group Litigation Order (GLO) Scheme, the Horizon Convictions Redress Scheme (HCRS) and the Horizon Shortfall Scheme Appeals (HSSA) process are progressing.

items.title: UK manufacturers to save millions of pounds a year as government removes offshore wind energy tariffs
items.summary: British manufacturers will save millions of pounds a year as the Government removes tariffs on offshore wind energy components from 1 April.
